# Source Card: Diagnosis and Management of Acquired Aplastic Anemia: Consensus Statement of Indian Academy of Pediatrics

## Summary
This is a peer-reviewed national society (Indian Academy of Pediatrics) consensus statement on diagnosing and managing acquired aplastic anemia (AA) in children, published in Indian Pediatrics (2022;59(6):467-475). For the pediatric CDS module it establishes source-supported facts about the diagnostic requirement (mandatory bone marrow biopsy), the required exclusion of mimics before diagnosis, a conservative transfusion target (hemoglobin >6 g/dL in children without co-morbidities), and the timing window for immunosuppressive-therapy response assessment (3-6 months). The laboratory count-based severity criteria (severe / very severe AA) referenced by the treatment recommendations were NOT locatable in an accessible verbatim passage and are recorded as a gap.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Bone marrow biopsy is mandatory for diagnosis — locator: Abstract, key recommendation (i) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:edea4879cff5fe27a83e56e01a24e4c43adfaeceb7bc5a7cd334602486745f7f]"
- (ev_002) Exclude IBMFS, connective-tissue disorders, viral infections, PNH, and drug/heavy-metal suppression in all cases — locator: Abstract, key recommendation (ii) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0eb7338f3db33cfb8501fe0edc678d8b2f606d18b822b2d5ff8c0b5a3d050bc2]"
- (ev_003) Conservative transfusion target of hemoglobin >6 g/dL in children without co-morbidities — locator: Abstract, key recommendation (iii) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c5a004730466a9fc3d0d1bf13146246b8d1965ef55a97345034c28c0ec64e045]"
- (ev_004) Wait 3-6 months for IST response assessment — locator: Abstract, key recommendation (vii) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c3e7df8fd122f18176c0ecdd052577e4b62439fbc10ef521c2b7c3c6299d6679]"
- (ev_005) HLA-matched sibling donor HSCT preferred first-line for very severe/severe AA; horse ATG + cyclosporine recommended IST — locator: Abstract, key recommendations (iv) and (vii) — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5b998cf203c230637fd3a197101b1f66ec75002b452c9075d559288237a2d5fb]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric ("children"); the statement does not give a numeric age band in the locatable text, and it is India-specific (resource-availability context, e.g. HSCT non-availability). Sex/GA-PNA not applicable to these recommendations.
- Assay/method: the one CBC-numeric threshold (Hb >6 g/dL, ev_003) is stated without analyzer/method qualification in the source. The 3-6 month IST window (ev_004) is a timing threshold, not an analyzer cutoff.
- Lifecycle: consensus finalized/approved in the October 2020 online meeting from a July 2016 Hyderabad draft; published 2022-02 (Indian Pediatr 2022;59:467-475). No retirement or superseding version stated in the source.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- ev_002 defines a diagnostic scope boundary: acquired AA should only be diagnosed after excluding inherited bone marrow failure syndromes, connective-tissue disorders, viral infections, PNH, and drug/heavy-metal marrow suppression — a differential-exclusion gate relevant to any pediatric pancytopenia CDS pathway. These are documented as the source's diagnostic gates, NOT as executable directives.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (numeric threshold not locatable): the count-based definitions of "severe" and "very severe" acquired AA (e.g., absolute neutrophil count, platelet count, reticulocyte count cutoffs, and bone-marrow cellularity percentage) that underpin recommendations (iv)/(vii) were NOT found in any accessible verbatim passage of this source (PMID 35105820) — the full text is paywalled (Springer/Indian Pediatrics) with no PMC copy. Per contract, these thresholds are therefore NOT asserted here. The commonly cited (modified Camitta) criteria (severe: ANC <0.5x10^9/L; very severe: ANC <0.2x10^9/L; platelets <20x10^9/L; reticulocytes low; hypocellular marrow) appear in other guidelines but must be confirmed against a different, quotable source before use.
- Treatment/transfusion items (ev_003, ev_005) are recorded as documentation of the source's stance, NOT as rf-issued diagnosis/treatment/transfusion directives; they are proposals for the CDS converter, not executable rules.
- Recency/scope: 2022 India-specific consensus; may conflict with region-specific transfusion or severity conventions in other national/ASH guidelines — any such conflict must be preserved and named against the specific competing source, never averaged.
